text: You can't see a black hole — so how did scientists capture its shadow? Meet the Event Horizon Telescope—an array of global telescopes acting as one gigantic eye. In 2019, they unveiled the first ever image of a black hole: M87*, showcasing its photon ring. This shadow isn’t the black hole itself, but the light bending around it—a cosmic illusion. The EHT Collaboration's groundbreaking work pushed the boundaries of our understanding of the universe. Curious about what else we can find in space? Hit that follow button for more!
